SIGNAL BOX Log today's study

Build your day from one or more topics — type the real hours for each (e.g. 1h Quantitative Methods + 3h Economics = 4h). The system totals your hours and awards points automatically. A study day opens the next station in Tube history, and the subway chime plays. Edit any day later in the Logbook.

🤝
Log honestly. This network is only as real as the hours you truly study. No one is checking — which is exactly why it counts. Inflated logs build a paper railway; honest ones build knowledge you can actually sit an exam on.

BRIEFING How the game works

You are London Underground Limited. Your mission: build the entire network — all 272 stations — by doing the real work toward your goal, whatever it is: an exam, a course, a project, a thesis, or a skill you're learning. Every honest day of focused work lays new track and opens the next station in the Tube's true history, from 1863 to today. Reach 272 and the Underground is crowned #1 in the world for public transport.

1
Log your study, build your day. In the Log tab, add one or more topics and type the real hours for each — e.g. 1h Quantitative Methods + 3h Economics. The system adds them into a daily total.
2
Hours become points, automatically. Your day's total hours fall into a scoring band (table below). You never pick points by hand — you log honest hours and the network awards funding.
3
Each study day opens a station. A day of 1 hour or more opens the next real station in chronological order — Paddington (1863) first, Battersea Power Station (2021) last — and the subway chime plays.
4
Track your service. The Progress tab shows points for today, this week, and this month, your streak, a 14-day chart, and hours per focus area. The Network tab shows every station you've opened.
5
Be honest — always. Nobody checks your logs, which is the whole point. A quiet or zero day costs 3 points and opens no station, and that is completely fine. Inflated logs build a paper railway; honest ones build knowledge you can sit an exam on.
6
Make it yours. In Settings you can rename your goal, edit your focus areas, change the scoring bands, and set the target. Everything you log can be edited or deleted later in the Logbook, and your progress saves automatically.
Total hours studied in a dayPoints
Below 1 hour / no study−3
1–2 hours+1
2–3 hours+3
3–4 hours+6
4–6 hours+9
6–8 hours+15
8+ hours+24

GALLERY Every line, mapped (2026)

The eleven lines of the Underground you're building, each as a current route diagram. They rotate automatically — each shown in full. Tap any diagram to view it enlarged.

Auto-rotates every 15 seconds · tap a dot or the arrows to browse

SETTINGS Make it yours

Your goal name appears on the victory screen when you complete the network.

Default is 272 — one for every real station on the Tube. Lower it for a shorter campaign.

These are the categories you log time against — subjects, project phases, skills, whatever fits your goal.

Each band sets the points awarded once your day's total hours reach its threshold (≥). The system picks the highest band your hours qualify for. Editing recalculates every past log automatically.

Wipe all logs, stations and settings. Cannot be undone.

London Underground · Study Network Builder
Build your network, one honest day at a time. Mind the gap, and good luck, Operator.
Progress saves automatically. Station opening years reflect each station's debut on what is now the Underground.
